title: Lucid Group (LCID) Balance Sheet Analysis
text: Lucid's balance sheet reflects a company in early stages of monetization with substantial asset accumulation but massive operating losses. Total assets include manufacturing facilities, inventory, and spare parts related to vehicle production. Current liabilities have grown as the company manages payables to suppliers. The company's total debt exceeds $3 billion while cash reserves have declined from peaks above $5 billion. The debt to equity ratio reflects heavy reliance on external funding to sustain operations. Working capital metrics show the company is managing supplier relationships but burning through capital at unsustainable rates. The balance sheet structure demonstrates that Lucid cannot sustain operations without external capital infusions, likely requiring continuous funding through 2028-2030 at minimum.
